replication_study.hypothesis: H1 (Replication): In the independent sample available, higher Openness to Experience scores (Oppenness_Total) will be positively associated with more Leadership-Shaping Experiences (Leader_Exp_Total).

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single wave; full file Popper Data for Correlations.csv
data_plan.access_details: File stored locally in /app/data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/

qualification.explanation: Dataset targets the same constructs with identical operationalisations (parcel/scale totals) as the original study yet was collected independently and has a different N, allowing a robustness test.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Contains identical composite variables Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total (see column list via get_dataset_columns) that map to Openness to Experience and Leadership Experiences used in the original SEM model (post_registration.json ‘outcome_variable’, ‘independent_variables’).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Sample size 215 vs original 286; original four companies (post_registration.json data.source description), whereas replication file is an un-specified but distinct data collection event in 2022 (see file Final R Code for Popper & Amit Replication_2022.pdf header). Thus respondents represent a new sample.

data_plan.notes: File has many empty ‘Unnamed:’ columns to be ignored. Primary analysis uses clean total-score variables; no nested structure.

steps: Load Popper Data for Correlations.csv from /app/data.
Inspect, drop any rows with missing values (listwise).
Estimate simple OLS regression of Leader_Exp_Total on Oppenness_Total with constant.
planned_method.models: Simple linear regression (OLS) and bivariate Pearson correlation.
planned_method.control_variables: None (mimics zero-order path reported in original).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Result considered supportive if coefficient is positive and p < .05 (two-tailed).

files.replicate_openness_leadership.py: Loads dataset, runs correlation and OLS as per planned method, prints JSON with Pearson r, coefficient, p-value, and N.

codebase.notes: Script expects data under /app/data with identical folder structure. No external R dependencies; fully Python.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare replicated regression coefficient magnitude and significance to original standardized beta 0.34 (direction, significance).
